steps.text: Lead the response to the outbreak of Zika.
Engage experts from multiple centers to respond to the Zika outbreak and protect pregnant women and their fetuses.
Perform activities related to Zika-related epidemiology, emergency response, monitoring and reporting of Zika cases, vector control, laboratory diagnostics, travel risk assessment, and communication.
Distribute assay reagents to US and international laboratories.
Provide leadership on public health guidance on clinical, personal protection, and laboratory topics related to Zika.
Activate CDC's Emergency Operations Center to manage Zika response.
Form teams to focus on different areas of the Zika response.
Deploy teams of experts to assist in public health responses in affected areas.
Diagnose Zika using the Trioplex Real-time RT-PCR Assay.
Control mosquitoes and prevent the spread of Zika in the continental United States.
Reduce mosquito populations and risk of Zika transmission in Wynwood, Florida.
Cement the connection between Zika virus infection during pregnancy and birth defects.
Provide guidance and information for travelers and Olympic athletes about Zika.
Provide Zika information to travelers in collaboration with the US Department of State.
